questions.question: Explain the difference between int[] arr and int arr[] in Java. Which is preferred and why?
keyPoints: Both syntaxes are valid in Java
int[] arr is preferred as it clearly indicates type is 'int array'
int arr[] is C-style syntax
Type-first approach improves readability
IDE support better with int[] arr syntax

questions.question: What are the default initial values when an array is created in Java? Explain with examples for different data types.
keyPoints: Numeric arrays initialize to 0
boolean arrays initialize to false
char arrays initialize to '\u0000' (null character)
Object reference arrays initialize to null
Example: int[] nums = new int[3]; // all zeros
Example: String[] names = new String[3]; // all null

questions.question: Write a Java program to find the sum of all elements in an integer array.
keyPoints: Initialize sum variable to 0
Iterate through array using for loop
Add each element to sum
Display the result
Use either traditional or enhanced for loop
Time complexity: O(n)

questions.question: What is the length property of an array? How is it different from the length() method of String class?
keyPoints: Arrays have length property (not method)
Returns number of elements in array
Syntax: arrayName.length
String uses length() method with parentheses
Both return count but syntactically different
Arrays: int[] arr = new int[5]; arr.length // 5
String: "Hello".length() // 5

questions.question: Explain the concept of ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException with an example.
keyPoints: Exception thrown when accessing invalid index
Valid indices: 0 to length-1
Example: int[] arr = {1,2,3}; arr[3] throws exception
Array has 3 elements indexed 0,1,2
Index 3 is fourth position which doesn't exist
Should always check index < array.length before access

questions.question: What is a two-dimensional array in Java? How is it different from a jagged array?
keyPoints: 2D array organized as rows and columns
Syntax: int[][] arr = new int[3][4]
Jagged array has variable row lengths
Jagged: arr[0] = new int[2]; arr[1] = new int[5];
Both stored as array of arrays in Java
2D arrays are actually arrays of 1D arrays

questions.question: Write a Java program to find the maximum and minimum element in an array.
keyPoints: Initialize max and min with first element
Iterate from index 1 to length-1
Compare each element with max/min
Update accordingly
Handle edge cases for null/empty arrays
O(n) time complexity with single pass

questions.question: Explain arrays as objects in Java. Discuss memory allocation, advantages of contiguous memory, and demonstrate with a code example showing array declaration, initialization, and element access.
keyPoints: Arrays are objects in Java, stored in heap memory
Contiguous memory allocation enables O(1) random access
Declaration: type[] arrayName;
Initialization: arrayName = new type[size];
Can combine declaration and initialization
Memory address calculated: baseAddress + (index * elementSize)
Example code showing all concepts
Reference variable in stack, object in heap
Default initialization happens when using new keyword

questions.question: Write a comprehensive Java program to perform linear search and binary search on an array. Explain the time and space complexity of both algorithms.
keyPoints: Linear search: check each element sequentially
Binary search: requires sorted array, divide and conquer
Linear search: O(n) time, O(1) space
Binary search: O(log n) time, O(1) space (iterative)
Implement both with proper input handling
Explain when to use each algorithm
Binary search can be recursive or iterative
Code must compile and run correctly

questions.question: Write a Java program to sort an array using bubble sort and selection sort algorithms. Explain the working of each algorithm with a trace example.
keyPoints: Bubble sort: adjacent element comparison and swapping
Selection sort: find minimum, place at beginning
Bubble sort: O(n²) time, stable sort
Selection sort: O(n²) time, not stable
Trace through small array showing each pass
Code for both algorithms
Comparison of when each is useful
Memory is O(1) for both

questions.question: Explain the difference between shallow copy and deep copy of an array in Java. Write code demonstrating array cloning and the System.arraycopy() method.
keyPoints: Shallow copy: copies references, not objects
Deep copy: creates new objects for each element
Array.clone() creates shallow copy for object arrays
System.arraycopy() for copying between arrays
Primitive arrays: clone creates independent copy
Object arrays: clone copies references only
Code demonstrating clone() and arraycopy()
Explain scenarios requiring deep copy
Time complexity: O(n) for both methods
